records.specific_details: Meeting TD Alan Dillon at the announcement of Circular Economy Innovation Grant Scheme recipients.
records.intended_results: To inform the DPOs in attendance about Habitat's social enterprise and secure a meeting with the Minister, which would include a visit to the newest Habitat ReStore in Glasnevin, Dublin 11.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment

lobbying_activities: Habitat Ireland's Chief Executive, Jenny Williams, attend the announcement event for Circular Economy Innovation Grant Scheme recipients at ACT (Accelerating Change Together)'s premises in Ballina, Mayo. When in attendance she spoke with Minister Alan Dillon (TD) about Habitat ReStore and how the social enterprise is having an impact for local people, communities and the planet.This has since been followed up with the Department and the minister will visit ReStore Glasnevin later this year. - Informal communication

records.specific_details: NCI Executive Coordinator Deirdre Lane met with Minister Dillon at the RDS where the RDS hosted its Circular Economy Alumni Day in Dublin. She mentioned the Funding report from NCI for the Ministers attention. She drew attention to 2026 being the UN year for Women in Agriculture for the Ministers consideration.
records.intended_results: Awareness of Natural Capital Ireland and Natural Capital accounting updates underway,

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment

lobbying_activities: The Minister was attending the RDS Circular Economy Alumni Day in Dublin where he delivered his address and Deirdre spoke with the minister as he was leaving the event afterwards. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Briefed Deputy Alan Dillon TD on the needs of the biomethane industry and requirements for the mobilisation of the sector including the needs for the immediate publication by the Government, of the Renewable heat Obligation (RHO) scheme deatils, as a key driver for the development of the sector.

records.specific_details: Mercosur Trade Deal
records.intended_results: That the Government and MEPs must honour their commitments to stand up for farmers and defend their interests by opposing the current Mercosur trade deal.::To discuss farmers' concerns with the Minister for Foreign Affairs::To reiterate strong opposition to the Mercosur agreement in its current form. The beef industry in Ireland supports 90,000 farmers and around 25,000 jobs and is critical to the rural economy. There are very strong concerns about the impact the EU-Mercosur trade agreement would have on the viability of some 90,000 families.::That, while it is often argued that an additional 99,000t of beef from the Mercosur block countries would represent only 1-2% of the EU beef market, this statistic is in fact very misleading. The high value EU steak market (fillet, striploin, and cube) will be targeted by Mercosur beef exporters and will impact EU producers' ability to achieve a positive return on the carcass. 30% of the value of the carcass is contained within the yield of high
